SG-001_close_state.mmd:
```
---
title: close_state (osaka/state.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_del_trie : del state._main_trie
    q_del_trie --> q_del_storage : del state._storage_tries
    q_del_storage --> q_del_snapshots : del state._snapshots
    q_del_snapshots --> q_final : del state.created_accounts
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_init
        INV-001: All state references are released after close_state
    end note
```

SG-002_begin_transaction.mmd:
```
---
title: begin_transaction (osaka/state.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_copy_main : copy_trie(state._main_trie)
    q_copy_main --> q_copy_storage : copy storage tries
    q_copy_storage --> q_snap_state : state._snapshots.append((main_copy, storage_copy))
    q_snap_state --> q_copy_transient : copy transient_storage tries
    q_copy_transient --> q_final : transient_storage._snapshots.append(transient_copy)
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_snap_state
        INV-002: Snapshot count increases by 1
        INV-003: Snapshots preserve deep copies
    end note
```

SG-003_commit_transaction.mmd:
```
---
title: commit_transaction (osaka/state.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_pop : state._snapshots.pop()
    q_pop --> q_check : evaluate snapshot count
    q_check --> q_clear : len(state._snapshots) == 0
    q_check --> q_transient : len(state._snapshots) > 0
    q_clear --> q_transient : state.created_accounts.clear()
    q_transient --> q_final : transient_storage._snapshots.pop()
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_pop
        INV-004: Snapshot count decreases by 1
        INV-005: created_accounts cleared only when last snapshot popped
    end note
```

SG-004_rollback_transaction.mmd:
```
---
title: rollback_transaction (osaka/state.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_restore : state._main_trie, state._storage_tries = state._snapshots.pop()
    q_restore --> q_check : evaluate snapshot count
    q_check --> q_clear : len(state._snapshots) == 0
    q_check --> q_transient : len(state._snapshots) > 0
    q_clear --> q_transient : state.created_accounts.clear()
    q_transient --> q_final : transient_storage._tries = transient_storage._snapshots.pop()
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_restore
        INV-006: State is restored to snapshot state after rollback
        INV-007: Transient storage is restored to snapshot after rollback
    end note
```

SG-005_get_account.mmd:
```
---
title: get_account (osaka/state.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_lookup : account = get_account_optional(state, address)
    q_lookup --> q_return_acct : isinstance(account, Account)
    q_lookup --> q_return_empty : not isinstance(account, Account)
    q_return_acct --> q_final : return account
    q_return_empty --> q_final : return EMPTY_ACCOUNT
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_lookup
        INV-008: get_account never returns None
    end note
```

    q_return_zero --> q_final : return U256(0)
    q_lookup --> q_assert : value = trie_get(trie, key)
    q_assert --> q_final : return value

SG-012_set_storage.mmd:
```
---
title: set_storage (osaka/state.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_assert : assert trie_get(state._main_trie, address) is not None
    q_assert --> q_get_trie : trie = state._storage_tries.get(address)
    q_get_trie --> q_create_trie : trie is None
    q_get_trie --> q_set : trie is not None
    q_create_trie --> q_assign_trie : trie = Trie(secured=True, default=U256(0))
    q_assign_trie --> q_set : state._storage_tries[address] = trie
    q_set --> q_check_empty : trie_set(trie, key, value)
    q_check_empty --> q_cleanup : trie._data == {}
    q_check_empty --> q_final : trie._data != {}
    q_cleanup --> q_final : del state._storage_tries[address]
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_assert
        INV-017: Account must exist before setting storage
        INV-018: Empty storage trie is cleaned up
        INV-019: Setting value to U256(0) effectively deletes the key
    end note
```

    q_compute --> q_final : return root(state._storage_tries[address])

SG-020_move_ether.mmd:
```

title: move_ether (osaka/state.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    q_init --> q_reduce : sender.balance >= amount

    q_reduce --> q_apply_sender : modify_state(state, sender_address, reduce_sender_balance)
    q_apply_sender --> q_increase : recipient.balance += amount
    q_increase --> q_final : modify_state(state, recipient_address, increase_recipient_balance)
    q_final --> [*]

```

## Summary

I extracted **12 program graphs** from the Osaka `transactions.py` specification, covering every functional unit in the module:

**Files generated** (all under `/home/gohan/ga-runners/actions-runner-security-agent-2/_work/security-agent/security-agent/outputs/graphs/W5B6_1770278556/osaka-transactions/`):

| ID | Function | File | Description |
|:---|:---------|:-----|:------------|
| SG-001 | `encode_transaction` | `SG-001_encode_transaction.mmd` | 5-way type dispatch encoding transactions to RLP or typed format with type prefix bytes 0x01-0x04 |
| SG-002 | `decode_transaction` | `SG-002_decode_transaction.mmd` | Inverse of encode -- dispatches on first byte prefix to decode typed transactions |
| SG-003 | `validate_transaction` | `SG-003_validate_transaction.mmd` | Sequential 4-check validation: gas sufficiency (EIP-7623 floor), nonce overflow (EIP-2681), initcode size, gas limit cap |
| SG-004 | `calculate_intrinsic_cost` | `SG-004_calculate_intrinsic_cost.mmd` | Multi-phase gas cost calculation: calldata tokenization, create cost, access list cost, authorization cost |
| SG-005 | `recover_sender` | `SG-005_recover_sender.mmd` | Most complex graph -- validates r/s bounds, dispatches on tx type for signature recovery with type-specific signing hashes |
| SG-006 | `signing_hash_pre155` | `SG-006_signing_hash_pre155.mmd` | Linear 3-node graph: assemble fields, RLP encode, keccak256 |
| SG-007 | `signing_hash_155` | `SG-007_signing_hash_155.mmd` | Same structure as pre-155 but appends (chain_id, 0, 0) for replay protection |
| SG-008 | `signing_hash_2930` | `SG-008_signing_hash_2930.mmd` | 4-node graph with 0x01 type prefix prepended before hashing |
| SG-009 | `signing_hash_1559` | `SG-009_signing_hash_1559.mmd` | 4-node graph with 0x02 type prefix, includes EIP-1559 fee fields |
| SG-010 | `signing_hash_4844` | `SG-010_signing_hash_4844.mmd` | 4-node graph with 0x03 type prefix, includes blob gas and versioned hashes |
| SG-011 | `signing_hash_7702` | `SG-011_signing_hash_7702.mmd` | 4-node graph with 0x04 type prefix, includes authorizations |
| SG-012 | `get_transaction_hash` | `SG-012_get_transaction_hash.mmd` | Simple 2-branch dispatch: RLP-encode legacy transactions or hash typed bytes directly |

Key observations:
- The signing hash functions (SG-006 through SG-011) form a family with increasing field sets and distinct type prefixes, reflecting Ethereum's transaction type evolution from pre-EIP-155 through EIP-7702.
- `validate_transaction` (SG-003) implements the EIP-7623 calldata floor price mechanism where gas must cover `max(intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor_gas_cost)`.
- `recover_sender` (SG-005) is the most complex graph with deep branching for legacy v-value handling (pre-155 vs EIP-155) and y_parity validation for typed transactions.

    q_calc_tokens --> q_calc_floor : tokens = zero_bytes + (len(tx.data) - zero_bytes) * 4
    q_calc_floor --> q_calc_data : calldata_floor = tokens * FLOOR_CALLDATA_COST + TX_BASE_COST
    q_calc_data --> q_check_create : data_cost = tokens * STANDARD_CALLDATA_TOKEN_COST
    q_check_create --> q_create_cost : tx.to == Bytes0
    q_check_create --> q_check_access : tx.to != Bytes0

---
title: validate_transaction (osaka/transactions.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_calc : intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor = calculate_intrinsic_cost(tx)
    q_calc --> q_check_gas : max(intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor) > tx.gas
    q_check_gas --> q_error_gas : gas insufficient
    q_check_gas --> q_check_nonce : gas sufficient
    q_error_gas --> q_final : raise InsufficientTransactionGasError
    q_check_nonce --> q_error_nonce : U256(tx.nonce) >= U256(U64.MAX_VALUE)
    q_check_nonce --> q_check_initcode : nonce valid
    q_error_nonce --> q_final : raise NonceOverflowError
    q_check_initcode --> q_error_initcode : tx.to == Bytes0 and len(tx.data) > MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE
    q_check_initcode --> q_check_gaslimit : initcode valid
    q_error_initcode --> q_final : raise InitCodeTooLargeError
    q_check_gaslimit --> q_error_gaslimit : tx.gas > TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT
    q_check_gaslimit --> q_return : gas limit valid
    q_error_gaslimit --> q_final : raise TransactionGasLimitExceededError
    q_return --> q_final : return (intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor)
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_calc
        INV-001: Gas must cover max(intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor) per EIP-7623
        INV-002: Nonce must be < U64.MAX_VALUE per EIP-2681

SG-004_calculate_intrinsic_cost.mmd:
```
---
title: calculate_intrinsic_cost (osaka/transactions.py)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init
    q_init --> q_count_zeros : zero_bytes = 0
    q_count_zeros --> q_iter_bytes : for byte in tx.data
    q_iter_bytes --> q_inc_zero : byte == 0
    q_iter_bytes --> q_calc_tokens : iteration complete
    q_inc_zero --> q_iter_bytes : zero_bytes += 1
    q_calc_tokens --> q_calc_floor : tokens = zero_bytes + (len(tx.data) - zero_bytes) * 4
    q_calc_floor --> q_calc_data : calldata_floor = tokens * FLOOR_CALLDATA_COST + TX_BASE_COST
    q_calc_data --> q_check_create : data_cost = tokens * STANDARD_CALLDATA_TOKEN_COST
    q_check_create --> q_create_cost : tx.to == Bytes0
    q_check_create --> q_check_access : tx.to != Bytes0
    q_create_cost --> q_check_access : create_cost = TX_CREATE_COST + init_code_cost
    q_check_access --> q_iter_access : tx has access_list
    q_check_access --> q_check_auth : tx has no access_list
    q_iter_access --> q_check_auth : sum(ACCESS_COST + slots * STORAGE_KEY_COST)
    q_check_auth --> q_return : tx is not SetCodeTransaction
    q_check_auth --> q_auth_cost : tx is SetCodeTransaction
    q_auth_cost --> q_return : auth_cost = PER_EMPTY_ACCOUNT_COST * len(authorizations)
    q_return --> q_final : return (TX_BASE_COST + data_cost + create_cost + access_cost + auth_cost, calldata_floor)
    q_final --> [*]

    note right of q_calc_tokens
        INV-001: TX_BASE_COST = 21000
        INV-002: Zero bytes = 1 token, non-zero = 4 tokens (EIP-7623)
        INV-003: FLOOR_CALLDATA_COST = 10 per token
        INV-004: Contract creation adds TX_CREATE_COST (32000)
    end note

    q_recover_typed --> q_address : secp256k1_recover(r, s, y_parity, signing_hash_<type>(tx))
    q_address --> q_final : return Address(keccak256(public_key)[12:32])
    q_final --> [*]
